rationale: I've worked hard all my life, but the gap keeps widening. The government should step in to help the working folks.
I'm a strong Democrat and believe the government has a responsibility to help the poor improve their standard of living.
As a lifelong Democrat, I believe government has a responsibility to address societal problems and ensure everyone has a fair shot. I think it should do more, not less.
I've seen both hard work and luck matter. Hard work is important, but being in the right place or having help can make a big difference too.
I'm a strong Republican and believe the government should not interfere with income redistribution. It's about personal responsibility, not government handouts.
Government shouldn't be handing out handouts; folks need to pull themselves up by their bootstraps like I did.
As a strong Republican, I firmly believe the government is overstepping its bounds and doing too much that should be left to individuals and businesses.
Hard work is the key to getting ahead. I've seen it my whole life—people who work hard succeed, and those who rely on luck or handouts don't.
I'm a Democrat, so I believe the government should help reduce inequality, but I've done well financially, so I'm not extreme.
I believe the government should help those in need, but I also think there should be limits.

I'm retired and rely on Social Security and Medicare, so I see the good government can do. But I also know high taxes can be a burden. Still, given my Democratic leanings, I think we need more government help to solve problems.
At my age, I've seen that putting in the effort and working hard is what got me where I am. Sure, some luck helps, but without hard work, you don't get ahead.
I'm struggling to make ends meet, so I think the government should help narrow the gap, but I'm not fully committed to extreme measures.
I’ve struggled myself, so I think the government should help, but I also believe people need to try. Leaning toward help.
I'm struggling to make ends meet, so I think the government should do more to help people like me, but I also believe in personal responsibility, so not too much.
I've worked hard my whole life but still struggle to get by; it's clear that luck and help from others make a big difference.
As a Democrat, I believe the government should help reduce inequality, but as someone with high income, I'm cautious about extreme measures.
I believe the government has a moral obligation to help those in need, especially the poor, to ensure a basic standard of living for everyone.
I believe the government should play a larger role in solving problems, especially for seniors like me.
I've worked hard my whole life, but I've also been lucky to have good opportunities and support. Both matter.
I worked hard for my money and don't think the government should take it to give to others. People should earn their own way.
As a high-income Republican, I believe people should take care of themselves without government handouts.
I believe the government is already too involved in our lives and should stay out of things that individuals and businesses can handle themselves.
I've worked hard to get where I am, and I believe that's what matters most.
As someone with a graduate degree but low income, I see the struggles of inequality firsthand. The government should act, but not too aggressively.
I've struggled financially myself, so I see the need for some government help, but I also believe in personal responsibility. A little support can go a long way.
I have a master's degree but my income is below average, so I think the government should do a bit more to help people like me, but I'm independent and don't want too much government overreach.
I have a master's degree and work hard, but my income is still modest, so I think both hard work and luck play a role.
I believe everyone has the opportunity to succeed through hard work; the government shouldn't redistribute wealth.
I'm a Republican and believe people should mostly help themselves, but I've struggled financially, so I understand some need for assistance, just not too much government.
As a Republican, I believe the government overreaches into private matters and should do less.
Hard work is the key to success; I believe people can overcome circumstances through effort.

As a Black woman and a Democrat, I strongly believe the government should reduce income inequality to help those struggling, including many in my community. Raising taxes on the wealthy makes sense to me.
As a Democrat, I believe the government has a responsibility to help the poor, especially in these tough times.
As a Black woman and Democrat, I believe the government should do more to address systemic inequality and help those in need.
Я верю, что и упорный труд, и удача с поддержкой важны. В моей жизни были и тяжёлый труд, и моменты, когда помогали другие или просто везло.
I work hard but it's tough. The rich have too much, but I don't want huge taxes either. A little help would be nice.
I've seen people struggle, and I think the government should help, but not do everything. Some personal responsibility is important.
I'm struggling to get by, and I think the government should step in more to help folks like me. But I'm not completely sure, so I won't go all the way to 1.
I've worked hard but still struggle, so I think both hard work and luck or help matter.
I've worked hard for what I have, and the government shouldn't be taking from the rich to give to others. It's not their job to fix income differences.
I believe people should take care of themselves; the government shouldn't be too involved.
Government interferes too much in our lives and should leave things to individuals and businesses.
I've made it this far through my own effort, and I believe that's how anyone gets ahead.
As a strong Democrat, I believe it's the government's responsibility to reduce income inequality, even though I'm well-off myself.
I strongly believe the government has a responsibility to help the poor; it's a core Democratic value.
As a strong Democrat, I believe the government should do more to address societal issues like healthcare and inequality.
I've worked hard to get where I am, but I also recognize that good breaks and supportive people have helped me along the way. Both matter.
As a low-income Democrat with an advanced degree, I strongly believe the government should reduce income inequality.
As a low-income person and strong Democrat, I believe it's the government's duty to help the poor.
As a strong Democrat with low income, I believe the government should do more to address inequality and help those in need.
With a master's degree but low income, I've seen that hard work isn't enough—luck and help from others are crucial. My party affiliation reinforces that systemic factors matter more.
As a low-income Democrat, I've lived the struggle. The government absolutely needs to reduce income differences—it's only fair.
I've struggled financially and believe the government should do more to help people like me.
As a low-income Democrat, I believe the government should do more to help people like me.
I've worked hard my whole life but still struggle. Without help from others and a bit of luck, it's nearly impossible to get ahead.
As a working-class woman with modest income, I strongly believe the government must step in to close the gap between rich and poor.
I think the government should do more to help the poor, but not necessarily everything possible. As a Democrat with limited income, I've seen how programs help people like me.
I think the government should do more to help people like me who struggle with low income and need support.
I think both hard work and luck or help from others matter. Hard work is important, but without opportunities or support, it's not enough.
